title: Microcos stylocarpa
taxonomy: Microcos stylocarpa is classified in the family Melastomataceae. This plant is a species of flowering plant. The classification also places it in the genus Microcos. Microcos stylocarpa is native to the tropical regions of Asia. It is a type of shrub or small tree.
uses: Microcos stylocarpa is a plant species that has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin infections, and digestive issues. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their potential antioxidant and anticancer effects. In traditional medicine, Microcos stylocarpa is used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory problems. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal potential and to standardize its use in modern medicine.
benefits: Microcos stylocarpa, a plant native to Southeast Asia, is known for its medicinal properties and potential health benefits. The plant contains various bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may contribute to its therapeutic effects. However, initial findings indicate that Microcos stylocarpa may hold promise as a natural remedy for certain health conditions.

The plant has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine to treat various health conditions. parts: Microcos stylocarpa is a plant species known for its medicinal properties. The leaves of the plant are commonly used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. Specifically, the leaves are used to treat fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. In addition, the plant's leaves are also used to treat skin conditions and wounds. preparations: Microcos stylocarpa is a plant species used in traditional medicine in various parts of Asia. side_effects: Microcos stylocarpa, also known as the Malaysian cherry, is a plant that has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine. intro: Microcos stylocarpa, commonly known as the Bunga Telang or Red Batai, is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Microcos stylocarpa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Microcos stylocarpa is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies such as infusions, tonics, and pastes.
